name: dns
context: Create, update, list, and delete DNS records for Cloudflare-managed zones via API and CLI
info: Cloudflare DNS provides authoritative DNS hosting with sub-10ms global resolution from 300+ edge locations. Zones represent domains added to a Cloudflare account. DNS records (A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT, SRV, etc.) are managed via the REST API or Wrangler. The orange cloud (proxied) toggle routes traffic through Cloudflare's CDN and security layer; grey cloud (DNS-only) resolves directly to the origin. Agents use this skill to point domains at Workers, Pages, and origin servers, configure email routing (MX, SPF, DKIM, DMARC), and verify domain ownership. The API is fully CRUD with list, create, update, patch, and delete operations on DNS records.
body:
# Cloudflare DNS

Cloudflare provides authoritative DNS with global anycast resolution under 10ms. Manage zones and records via API or CLI.

## Concepts

- **Zone** — A domain (e.g., example.com) managed by Cloudflare
- **Record** — A DNS entry (A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT, etc.) within a zone
- **Proxied (orange cloud)** — Traffic routes through Cloudflare CDN/WAF
- **DNS-only (grey cloud)** — DNS resolves directly to origin IP

## Adding a Domain

```bash
# Add a zone via API
curl -X POST "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data '{"name":"example.com","type":"full"}'
# Then update nameservers at your registrar to Cloudflare's assigned NS
```

## Managing DNS Records

### Create Records

```bash
# A record — point domain to an IP
curl -X POST "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/$ZONE_ID/dns_records" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data '{
    "type": "A",
    "name": "app",
    "content": "192.0.2.1",
    "ttl": 1,
    "proxied": true
  }'

# CNAME record — alias to another domain
curl -X POST "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/$ZONE_ID/dns_records" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data '{
    "type": "CNAME",
    "name": "www",
    "content": "app.example.com",
    "ttl": 1,
    "proxied": true
  }'

# TXT record — domain verification, SPF, etc.
curl -X POST "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/$ZONE_ID/dns_records" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data '{
    "type": "TXT",
    "name": "@",
    "content": "v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com ~all",
    "ttl": 1
  }'

# MX record — email routing
curl -X POST "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/$ZONE_ID/dns_records" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data '{
    "type": "MX",
    "name": "@",
    "content": "mail.example.com",
    "priority": 10,
    "ttl": 1
  }'
```

### List Records

```bash
# List all DNS records for a zone
curl "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/$ZONE_ID/dns_records" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN"

# Filter by type
curl "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/$ZONE_ID/dns_records?type=A" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN"

# Filter by name
curl "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/$ZONE_ID/dns_records?name=app.example.com" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN"
```

### Update a Record

```bash
# Update (full replace)
curl -X PUT "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/$ZONE_ID/dns_records/$RECORD_ID" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data '{
    "type": "A",
    "name": "app",
    "content": "198.51.100.1",
    "ttl": 1,
    "proxied": true
  }'

# Patch (partial update)
curl -X PATCH "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/$ZONE_ID/dns_records/$RECORD_ID" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data '{"content": "198.51.100.2"}'
```

### Delete a Record

```bash
curl -X DELETE "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/$ZONE_ID/dns_records/$RECORD_ID" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN"
```

## Finding Your Zone ID

```bash
# List zones to find zone ID
curl "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones?name=example.com" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN" | jq '.result[0].id'
```

## Node.js SDK

```javascript
import Cloudflare from 'cloudflare';

const cf = new Cloudflare({ apiToken: process.env.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN });

// List zones
const zones = await cf.zones.list({ name: 'example.com' });
const zoneId = zones.result[0].id;

// Create A record
const record = await cf.dns.records.create({
  zone_id: zoneId,
  type: 'A',
  name: 'api',
  content: '192.0.2.1',
  proxied: true,
});

// List records
const records = await cf.dns.records.list({ zone_id: zoneId });

// Delete record
await cf.dns.records.delete(record.id, { zone_id: zoneId });
```

## Common Patterns

### Point Domain to Workers

Use a CNAME record or Workers route. For Workers custom domains:
```bash
# Workers custom domain (automatic DNS)
# In wrangler.toml:
# routes = [{ pattern = "api.example.com", custom_domain = true }]
```

### Point Domain to Pages

Add a custom domain in Dashboard > Pages > project > Custom domains. Cloudflare auto-creates the CNAME record.

### Email Setup (Google Workspace)

```bash
# MX records
# Priority 1: aspmx.l.google.com
# Priority 5: alt1.aspmx.l.google.com
# Priority 5: alt2.aspmx.l.google.com

# SPF
# TXT @ "v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com ~all"

# DKIM
# TXT google._domainkey "v=DKIM1; k=rsa; p=..."

# DMARC
# TXT _dmarc "v=DMARC1; p=quarantine; rua=mailto:dmarc@example.com"
```

sections.title: DNS record CRUD
sections.body: Create records with POST to `/zones/{zone_id}/dns_records` with `type`, `name`, `content`, `ttl`, and `proxied`. List with GET. Update with PUT (full replace) or PATCH (partial). Delete with DELETE. Record types: A (IPv4), AAAA (IPv6), CNAME (alias), MX (email), TXT (verification/SPF), SRV (service), NS (nameserver). TTL=1 means automatic (Cloudflare-managed).

sections.title: Proxied vs DNS-only
sections.body: Set `proxied: true` (orange cloud) to route traffic through Cloudflare's CDN, WAF, and DDoS protection. The origin IP is hidden. Set `proxied: false` (grey cloud) for DNS-only resolution — traffic goes directly to the origin. MX, TXT, and SRV records cannot be proxied. A and CNAME records support proxying.

sections.title: Finding zone and record IDs
sections.body: Get zone ID by listing zones filtered by name: `GET /zones?name=example.com`. Get record IDs by listing records: `GET /zones/{zone_id}/dns_records`. The zone ID also appears in the Cloudflare dashboard URL: `dash.cloudflare.com/{account_id}/{zone_name}` — the zone ID is in the Overview sidebar.

sections.title: Email DNS records
sections.body: Email requires MX records (mail routing), SPF via TXT (sender verification), DKIM via TXT (message signing), and DMARC via TXT (policy enforcement). MX records have priority values — lower numbers are tried first. SPF example: `v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com ~all`. DMARC example: `v=DMARC1; p=quarantine`.

links.context: Point domains to Workers via routes

related.note: Routes connect Workers to domains managed in DNS
Pages custom domains auto-create DNS records
Vercel also manages DNS for deployed projects

scripts.name: DNS Operations
scripts.description: Create, list, update, and delete DNS records for Cloudflare zones

ZONE_ID.description: Cloudflare zone ID for the target domain

depends.reason: Zone:DNS:Edit permission required for DNS management
